title: Narrow leadership & the index
question: When mega-caps carry the index and breadth thins, is that bearish — and can you time it?
finding: No warning survives. Across % above 200/50-DMA, the A/D line, new-highs-minus-lows, McClellan and cap-vs-equal momentum, the signs run the wrong way (high breadth weakly precedes lower returns), every conditional 63-day difference is bootstrap-insignificant, and every cash-on-warning overlay trails buy-and-hold on total return out-of-sample (the two that improve Sharpe give up return and fail robustness). The lone residue (% above 200-DMA de-risking) is one-crash evidence.
